From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Reachable part of the possible market

[![Image 5: icon](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Edit-copy_purple-wikit.svg/40px-Edit-copy_purple-wikit.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Edit-copy_purple-wikit.svg)This article **appears to be a [dictionary definition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Wikipedia_is_not_a_dictionary "Wikipedia:Wikipedia is not a dictionary")**. Please help [expand it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Serviceable_available_market&action=edit) to present the subject [encyclopedically](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Encyclopedic_style "Wikipedia:Encyclopedic style")._(May 2023)_
[![Image 6](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/TAM-SAM-Market.jpg/330px-TAM-SAM-Market.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:TAM-SAM-Market.jpg)

[Total Available Market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Total_addressable_market "Total addressable market") (TAM), Serviceable (or Served) Available Market, and [Target Market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Target_market "Target market").

**Serviceable addressable market** (**SAM**; also **served available market**) is the part of the [total addressable market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Total_addressable_market "Total addressable market") (TAM) that can actually be reached.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serviceable_available_market#cite_note-The-Startup-Owners-Manual-2012-1)

## Overview

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Serviceable_available_market&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

The serviceable available market or served addressable market is more clearly defined as that market opportunity that exists within a firm's existing core competencies and/or past performance. The biggest consideration when calculating SAM is that a firm most likely can only service markets that are [core](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Core_business "Core business") or directly adjacent to its current customer base. As examples:

*   Consider a firm that plans to become a niche solution provider in the [Healthcare market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Healthcare_industry "Healthcare industry"). It is unlikely that the firm will quickly be able to capture market share in [manufacturing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manufacturing "Manufacturing") without significant investments in market expansion.
*   In [telecommunications](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telecommunication "Telecommunication") where a [service provider](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Service_provider "Service provider") is expanding into a new market that is [dominated](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dominance_(economics) "Dominance (economics)") by an incumbent that has existing infrastructure investments in place, the incumbent raises the barriers to entry in that market and therefore [market analysis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Market_analysis "Market analysis") will not count that market as serviceable.

## See also

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Serviceable_available_market&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]

*   [Total addressable market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Total_addressable_market "Total addressable market")
*   [Market segmentation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Market_segmentation "Market segmentation")
*   [Target market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Target_market "Target market")
